input:
When the Prophet saw the necklace he was deeply touched. He said to his companions: “If you feel it proper to release her prisoner for her and refund her her money, you may do so.” They said: “Of course” and released Abū al-ʿĀṣ, taking no ransom from him. Among the prisoners of Badr was also the Prophet’s own uncle al-ʿAbbās. He was the one the Prophet ordered not to be killed in the battle. Now the Muslims wanted him to pay his ransom.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 18: In Madinah After Badr

output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 18: In Madinah After Badr

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Abū al-ʿĀṣ
characters.appearance: Mid-30s, dark olive skin, athletic build. Unshaven, wearing a simple white tunic, brown woolen cloak, with rope around wrists. No headwear, dirty and worn-out sandals.

characters.name: al-ʿAbbās ibn Abd al-Muttalib
characters.appearance: Middle-aged, light olive skin, strong build. Thin grey-streaked beard, neatly kept. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Distinguished by slight henna tint on beard and subtle warm light.

output.moral_lesson: The Prophet teaches us the value of mercy and compassion, even towards those who have wronged us.
output.narrative_text: When the Prophet saw a beautiful necklace, he was deeply moved and said: “If you feel it right to release her prisoner and return her money, do so.” The Muslims agreed and freed Abū al-ʿĀṣ without any ransom. The Prophet's uncle, al-ʿAbbās, who was also a prisoner, would not be killed due to the Prophet's orders, but now the Muslims wanted him to pay his ransom.

compliance.notes: Prisoners shown in silhouette or full, without detailed facial expressions. No graphic violence or harm shown. Scene suitable for children.

output.image_prompt: Watercolor illustration of a quiet Madinah home after the Battle of Badr. A man in white thobe and green cloak, standing at a doorframe, seen from behind. Inside, a dark-skinned prisoner in simple white tunic and brown cloak, seen in silhouette, with ropes around his wrists. Nearby, a middle-aged man with light olive skin, grey-streaked beard, off-white thobe and brown cloak, sitting on a woven mat, with a concerned expression. Simple mud-brick walls, clay water jug in corner. Warm afternoon sunlight streaming through the doorway. No modern objects.
